nodes.label: Production Intelligence Positioning
nodes.rationale: AI never makes creative or final business decisions — it drafts, extracts, scores, and suggests; the founder always reviews and decides. This rule is applied consistently across every AI feature in the repo, distinguishing Atelier from an 'AI design generator'.

nodes.label: Frontend/Backend Architecture Split
nodes.rationale: The frontend talks to Supabase directly for all data (products, designs, vendors, quotes, etc.), protected by Row Level Security, so no backend round-trip is needed for CRUD. The Express backend (api/) exists only for calls that need a secret key that can't live in browser code (Gemini, Tavily, Stripe, Shopify, Resend).

nodes.label: percent() Double-Multiplication Bug
nodes.rationale: Every caller of lib/format.js's percent() already passed an already-x100 margin value, but the function multiplied by 100 again, causing ProductInsights.jsx's Retail Margin stat to render as e.g. '6500%' instead of '65%'. Fixed while building Financial Tools.

nodes.label: sales_data Missing platform Column Bug
nodes.rationale: sales_data's unique constraint never included a platform column, so a WooCommerce sale and a Shopify sale for the same product/month would silently overwrite each other. Fixed in migration 025 by adding a platform column and widening the unique constraint to (brand_id, product_id, month, platform).

nodes.label: disconnectStore() Cross-Platform Wipe Bug
nodes.rationale: disconnectStore() used to delete all of a brand's sales_data regardless of platform, so disconnecting Shopify would also wipe WooCommerce's revenue history. Now scoped to only the platform being disconnected.

nodes.label: Discarded OAuth Token Bug
nodes.rationale: The Instagram/TikTok connect flow ran a genuine OAuth handshake but connectAccount() never read the returned access token, so every 'connected' account had no real credential behind it. Fixed by rebuilding on the shared OAuth handoff helper and persisting the token to social_accounts.access_token.

nodes.label: Materials Delete RLS Gap
nodes.rationale: materials only had a select-only RLS policy before migration 020 — deleteMaterial() had nothing to actually delete with, since insert/update/delete RLS policies on materials didn't exist yet.

nodes.label: production_payments Schema Drift
nodes.rationale: production_payments had been read from and written to by Financial Tools' Cash Flow/Manufacturing Cost History and the Production Order Payments/break-even math since an earlier session, but no migration had ever created the table — the same schema-drift pattern later seen with content_posts/social_accounts. Fixed by migration 023.
